# Integration Checklist: Command, workflow, manifest, and file-layout reference

**Purpose**: Validate that DOC-007 requirements and planning artifacts define the source-data integrations for generated reference pages, source allowlists and exclusions, source-vs-dist responsibilities, docs-site script handoff, check-mode behavior, and existing-doc deep links.
**Created**: 2026-06-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are source allowlist and exclusion requirements specified as normalized repo-relative paths so a checkout under a `.worktrees/` parent is not incorrectly excluded? [Resolved, Spec FR-014, Plan Source Allowlist And Evidence Boundary]
- [x] CHK002 Is each generated reference page mapped to the source inputs that may provide source facts, inferred notes, and navigation evidence? [Resolved, Plan Generated Page Source-Input Matrix]
- [ ] CHK003 Are source citation requirements defined for source facts from `speckit-pro/`, `dist/claude/`, `dist/codex/`, manifests, scripts, tests, and docs-site navigation files? [Completeness, Spec FR-003, FR-014]
- [x] CHK004 Are checked-in `dist/claude/` and `dist/codex/` files scoped as generated-payload inventory evidence rather than authoring source-of-truth evidence for plugin semantics? [Resolved, Spec Validation And Handoff Boundaries, Plan Source Allowlist And Evidence Boundary]
- [ ] CHK005 Are generated reference outputs under `docs-site/src/content/docs/reference/*.md` excluded from source evidence while remaining writable output targets? [Completeness, Spec FR-014, Contract Boundaries]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Are source fact, source citation, and inferred-note fields clearly separated for every generated row so generator inputs cannot silently become uncited prose? [Clarity, Spec FR-003, FR-005, Data Model ReferenceRecord]
- [x] CHK007 Is source-vs-dist responsibility defined with an explicit mapping across skills, agents, manifests, hooks, scripts, tests, and generated payload layout? [Resolved, Plan Source-vs-Dist Responsibility Mapping]
- [ ] CHK008 Are optional or absent repository surfaces required to be omitted or labeled absent without invented facts? [Clarity, Spec Edge Cases, Data Model PlatformMapping]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the public GitHub citation URL shape defined consistently with repo-relative visible link text? [Clarity, Spec FR-003, Contract Content Contract]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Do source allowlist requirements align between the spec, research rationale, data model, contract, quickstart, and plan constraints? [Consistency, Spec FR-014, Research Source Inventory, Data Model SourceAllowlist]
- [x] CHK011 Are docs-site package scripts and `validate` handoff requirements defined with exact script behavior that matches the existing docs-site script model? [Resolved, Plan Docs-Site Script And Navigation Handoff]
- [ ] CHK012 Do check-mode exit codes and stale-output behavior align across the spec, contract, quickstart, and success criteria? [Consistency, Spec FR-013, SC-004, SC-005, Contract Check]
- [ ] CHK013 Does the `.github/workflows/*` exclusion align with the DOC-010 handoff and local-only docs validation requirement? [Consistency, Spec FR-022, Plan Constraints]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK014 Can reviewers objectively verify that 100% of source-fact rows cite existing allowlisted checked-in source paths? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-002]
- [ ] CHK015 Can reviewers objectively verify that check mode leaves the working tree unchanged for stale generated output? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-005, Contract Check]
- [x] CHK016 Are existing docs-page deep-link requirements mapped to exact source files and generated reference targets so generic-only `/reference/` links can be rejected? [Resolved, Plan Deep-Link Update Scope]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are missing, malformed, and absent optional source-input scenarios defined well enough for the later error-handling checklist to classify failure behavior? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK018 Are stale generated output scenarios separated from source/parsing/internal error scenarios in check mode? [Coverage, Spec FR-013, Contract Check]
- [ ] CHK019 Are docs-site navigation integration requirements defined for the existing `Reference` sidebar group and generated subpage ordering? [Coverage, Spec FR-019, Plan Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK020 Are future CI, broader link hardening, accessibility automation, search, and DOC-008/DOC-009 content depth excluded from this integration slice? [Scope, Spec FR-018, FR-022]

## Verification Rerun - 2026-06-17

- [ ] CHK021 Are repo-relative allowlist/exclusion requirements now explicit enough to avoid rejecting a valid checkout because its absolute parent path contains `.worktrees/`? [Resolved, Spec FR-014, Plan Source Allowlist And Evidence Boundary]
- [ ] CHK022 Does the generated page source-input matrix now define source-data coverage for all seven generated reference pages? [Resolved, Plan Generated Page Source-Input Matrix]
- [ ] CHK023 Are checked-in `dist/claude/` and `dist/codex/` files now explicitly scoped to generated-payload inventory evidence only? [Resolved, Spec Validation And Handoff Boundaries, Plan Source Allowlist And Evidence Boundary]
- [ ] CHK024 Does the source-vs-dist mapping now cover skills, agents, manifests, hooks, scripts, tests, and docs reference output with editability/classification rules? [Resolved, Plan Source-vs-Dist Responsibility Mapping]
- [ ] CHK025 Are `reference:generate`, `reference:check`, `validate`, and Reference sidebar handoff requirements now exact enough for implementation? [Resolved, Plan Docs-Site Script And Navigation Handoff]
